hints.Apud: 'Apud' is a preposition meaning 'among' — it governs the accusative
hints.Helvetios: 'Helvetios' is a proper noun in the accusative plural — the object of 'apud'
hints.Orgetorix: 'Orgetorix' is a proper noun — the subject of this sentence

hints.Present: Present of 'esse' 3rd person would be 'est' — 'fuit' uses the perfect stem 'fu-'
hints.Imperfect: Imperfect of 'esse' 3rd person would be 'erat' — 'fuit' uses the perfect stem 'fu-'
hints.Pluperfect: Pluperfect of 'esse' 3rd person would be 'fuerat' — 'fuit' lacks the -era- marker

hints.fio, fieri, factus sum, —: These are the principal parts of 'fieri' (to become/be made)
hints.eo, ire, ii, itum: These are the principal parts of 'ire' (to go)
hints.possum, posse, potui, —: These are the principal parts of 'posse' (to be able)

hints.Nominative: Nominative plural would be 'Helvetii' — 'Helvetios' has the -os accusative plural ending
hints.Genitive: Genitive plural would be 'Helvetiorum' — 'Helvetios' has the -os accusative ending
hints.Ablative: Ablative plural would be 'Helvetiis' — 'Helvetios' has the -os accusative ending

steps.target: It is governed by the preposition 'apud'
choices: It is the direct object of 'fuit'
It is governed by the preposition 'apud'
It is the subject of the sentence

hints.It is the direct object of 'fuit': 'fuit' (was) is intransitive and does not take a direct object
hints.It is the subject of the sentence: The subject is 'Orgetorix' — 'Helvetios' is governed by the preposition 'apud'
hints.It is in apposition with 'Orgetorix': Apposition requires the same case — 'Orgetorix' is nominative, 'Helvetios' is accusative

hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'nobilissimus' would be 'nobilissimi' — 'nobilissimus' is the nominative form
hints.Accusative: Accusative of 'nobilissimus' would be 'nobilissimum' — 'nobilissimus' is nominative
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'nobilissimus' would be 'nobilissimo' — 'nobilissimus' is nominative

hints.Positive: The positive form would be 'nobilis' — the -issimus suffix marks the superlative
hints.Comparative: The comparative form would be 'nobilior' — the -issimus suffix marks the superlative
hints.Diminutive: Diminutive is not a degree of adjectives — the -issimus suffix marks the superlative

hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'Orgetorix' would be 'Orgetorigis' — 'Orgetorix' is the nominative form
hints.Accusative: Accusative of 'Orgetorix' would be 'Orgetorigem' — 'Orgetorix' is nominative as the subject
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'Orgetorix' would be 'Orgetorige' — 'Orgetorix' is the nominative form

translation: Among the Helvetii Orgetorix was the noblest
